metadata.seed: I found out that my boss has not been paying me accurately. I am so upset because I don't know what to do. I feel like I should quit.

patient.query: I found out that my boss has not been paying me accurately. I am so upset because I don't know what to do. I feel like I should quit.

automatic_thoughts: I don't know what to do
I should quit

contradiction: Not involved in dealing with own monies (finding)

llm_response.response: "I'm really sorry to hear that you're feeling upset about the pay issue. Can you tell me more about what evidence you have that things aren't going well? It might help us figure out some steps to take together."

patient.query: Well, I guess I just keep looking at my paycheck and thinking about how much effort I put in at work. It feels like no matter how hard I try, it’s never enough. I see my colleagues getting raises and it makes me feel... I don’t know, like I’m not valued or something. It’s frustrating.

automatic_thoughts: no matter how hard I try, it’s never enough
I’m not valued

llm_response.response: "It sounds like you're feeling frustrated because you feel like your efforts aren't being recognized. Can you help me understand more about what evidence you have that you'll fail? And how does this thought make you feel?"

patient.query: Yeah, I mean, I see the way my boss interacts with others and it just seems like they get all the praise. Sometimes it feels like I'm invisible. When I think about failing, I just get this sinking feeling in my stomach like I’m going to be stuck in this dead-end job forever or worse, that I’ll lose it completely. It really makes me anxious.

automatic_thoughts: I'm invisible
I'm going to be stuck in this dead-end job forever
I'll lose my job completely

neutral: Fear of speaking to people in authority (finding)

patient.query: I guess it’s just hard to shake off those thoughts, you know? I mean, deep down, I want to be proud of what I do, but it’s like I’m always looking over my shoulder, worrying about what others think of me. Sometimes it feels like all this pressure is just building up and I don’t know how to release it. It’s exhausting.

want to be proud of what I do
always looking over my shoulder

neutral: Anxiety about saying the wrong thing (finding)

patient.query: Yeah, it does feel heavy, doesn’t it? I think there’s this part of me that always wants to prove I’m good enough, especially at work. I just hate the idea of being seen as a failure, like if I don’t meet expectations, I won’t be taken seriously. It just gnaws at me. Sometimes I feel I'd rather stay quiet than risk messing up. It’s... it's paralyzing.

automatic_thoughts: I need to prove I'm good enough
If I don't meet expectations, I won't be taken seriously

neutral: Fear of being a burden to others (finding)

patient.query: I mean, if I mess up... I guess I worry people will think I’m incompetent, or worse, that they’ll lose trust in me. I start imagining all these scenarios where I’m the one everyone talks about behind my back. It’s terrifying to think I might let everyone down, and then I just spiral into this panic. It’s like I can’t escape that feeling of judgment.

automatic_thoughts: I worry people will think I’m incompetent
They’ll lose trust in me
I’m the one everyone talks about behind my back
I might let everyone down

Like, maybe I could breathe a little easier without that constant weight of fear. But it’s tough to believe that could ever be true.

patient.query: Yeah, it definitely feels like a cycle. I notice it gets worse when I have deadlines or when I'm in meetings. I start doubting my ideas or worrying about how I’ll come across, and that just fuels the anxiety. It’s like I’m on high alert, just waiting for someone to point out what I’m doing wrong. I guess I never really thought about where it all comes from, but it feels like it’s been a part of me for so long.

automatic_thoughts: doubting my ideas
worrying about how I’ll come across
waiting for someone to point out what I’m doing wrong

patient.query: I don’t know... I guess there’s this voice in my head that tells me I have to be perfect all the time. Like if I’m not the best at what I do, I’m just not good enough. It’s hard not to listen to it, you know? It makes me feel like I’ve got to keep up this facade or I’ll crumble. It’s just really tiring trying to make sure everyone sees me in that light.

If I'm not the best, I'm not good enough
I have to keep up this facade
